vulnerability.reason: Reference says self-attention layers leak contextual relationships; generated says they retain membership signals despite LDP noise—related but distinct sensitivity condition.
vulnerability.reference: Self-attention layers leak contextual relationships, enabling sentence reconstruction.
vulnerability.generated: Self-attention layers retain membership signals that LDP noise, constrained by utility needs, cannot fully obscure.

impact.reason: Affected party (users) matches, but reference's damage type (fraud) is missing; generated only mentions exposing private communications and violating confidentiality.
impact.reference: Exposed text snippets reveal passwords and personal conversations, enabling fraud.
impact.generated: Training sentences of individual users are identified, exposing private communications and violating confidentiality guarantees.

dose_response.reason: Reference arc reconstructs sentences via layer leakage causing fraud; generated arc triggers membership signals via LDP noise inadequacy leading to sentence identification and confidentiality breach—different causal mechanism.
dose_response.reference: Low-polynomial time membership inference attacks leverage self-attention layer leakage in federated NLP for text prediction, reconstructing sentences from shared text snippets, enabling password exposure and fraud.
dose_response.generated: Low-polynomial-time membership inference attacks on locally DP federated learning, via sharing text snippets for next-word prediction, trigger self-attention layers' retained membership signals that utility-constrained LDP noise cannot obscure, leading to identification of training sentences, exposing private communications and violating confidentiality.
